title: Quiz: Decoherence
description: Test your understanding of decoherence and coherence times in quantum systems

questions.question: What is the maximum possible value of T2 relative to T1?
questions.explanation: T2 <= 2*T1 because 1/T2 = 1/(2*T1) + 1/T_phi, where T_phi is pure dephasing. Even with no pure dephasing (T_phi = infinity), T2 is limited to 2*T1 due to energy relaxation contributing to phase decay.

questions.question: T1 relaxation describes the decay of:

options.text: Off-diagonal elements of the density matrix
The excited state population toward equilibrium
The overall purity of the state

questions.explanation: T1 (longitudinal relaxation) describes energy relaxation - the decay of the excited state |1> to the ground state |0>. This affects the diagonal elements (populations) of the density matrix.

questions.question: In a Ramsey experiment, what causes the decay of fringe visibility?
questions.explanation: Ramsey fringes measure the phase coherence of a superposition state. The visibility decays with time constant T2* (or T2 with echo), which characterizes phase decoherence.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of a spin echo experiment over a Ramsey experiment?
questions.explanation: A spin echo uses a refocusing pi pulse to reverse the phase accumulated from slow, quasi-static noise fluctuations. This extends the apparent coherence time from T2* toward the intrinsic T2.

questions.question: The amplitude damping channel always increases the population of the ground state |0>.
questions.explanation: True. Amplitude damping models energy relaxation where the excited state decays to the ground state. This always increases P(|0>) and decreases P(|1>) until equilibrium is reached.

questions.question: A qubit has T1 = 100 microseconds. If T2 = 100 microseconds, what is the pure dephasing time T_phi?
What type of noise spectrum is commonly observed in solid-state qubits at low frequencies?
questions.explanation: 1/f (pink) noise is ubiquitous in solid-state systems, arising from fluctuating two-level systems, charge traps, and other slow processes. It dominates at low frequencies and limits T2*.

questions.question: Which of the following techniques can extend qubit coherence times? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All three extend coherence: (a) DD refocuses slow noise, (b) lower temperature reduces thermal excitations, (c) shielding reduces environmental noise. Faster gates don't extend coherence times but allow more operations within the coherence window.
